title.emphasis: Part 3: The Cooperative Compliance Test – Is it a "Safe" Cooperative Security?

section.para: If an instrument is determined to be a security under either Reves or Howey, it must then be tested for its compliance with the core principles of cooperative operation.

listitem.para: Subordination of Capital as Defense of the Commons: Does the security's structure ensure that financial gains are socialized for the benefit of the entire patronage, rather than being privately captured by the instrument holder?
Democratic Member Control: Is the instrument's ownership separated from governance, preserving the "one-member, one-vote" principle?

Patronage-Source Income Integrity (The "Actually Facilitates" Test): Is the security engineered so its function "actually facilitates" the cooperative's core mission, ensuring income generated is defensibly patronage-sourced?
